page.title: SAML IdP — Service Providers
page.description: Qeet ID acts as the SAML 2.0 Identity Provider here. Each Service Provider below trusts this IdP and receives signed assertions for its users.

metadata.title: IdP metadata
metadata.description: Service providers import this single metadata document to trust Qeet ID as their IdP. Hand this URL to each SP’s SAML configuration.
metadata.hint: Most SPs accept a metadata URL directly; for those that need the XML, point them at the same endpoint — it serves the signed IdP descriptor (entity ID, SSO URL, signing certificate).

list.title: Service providers
list.emptyDescription: Add the apps that should accept SSO from this IdP.

delete.title: Remove this service provider?
delete.descriptionNamed: {{name}} will no longer accept SSO from this IdP. This can’t be undone.
delete.descriptionFallback: It will no longer accept SSO from this IdP.

form.description: Enter the SP’s SAML entity ID and Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL — both come from the SP’s own metadata.
form.name: Name
form.acsUrlHelp: Where signed assertions are POSTed back.
form.nameIdAttributeHelp: Optional. Which user attribute populates the NameID.
form.certificateHelp: Optional. Required only if the SP signs its requests.
